title: Ambedkar's political philosophy: a grammar of public life from the social margins [Book Review]
author: Jha

opening_move.description: The review opens by diagnosing a 'thin interpretation' problem in Ambedkar studies: existing literature is biographical or historical rather than analytically philosophical, and relies on a narrow canon of his texts.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: A full analytical account of Ambedkar's political philosophy — drawn from his complete corpus — is needed to understand Ambedkarism as a praxial philosophy of the human condition

theoretical_positioning.description: The review endorses Rodrigues's analytical approach as filling a gap in Ambedkar scholarship, presenting its contributions appreciatively.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: intellectual history; close reading of Ambedkar's texts

case_type.case_name: Ambedkar's writings; Rodrigues, Ambedkar's Political Philosophy (2024)

normative_stake.domain: democratic theory / postcolonial theory / social justice

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. Author name listed without first name in PDF; likely Priyanka Jha.
